title: Review Queue UI shipped via the tree-integrated implementation (parallel build won the merge)
context: Corrects dec_92a6bacebf, which recorded a webview-panel implementation that was discarded during `git pull`. Two agents built the same roadmap item concurrently; the remote's tree-integrated version was the superset (added inline Edit and an auto-review harness the webview lacked), so the merge resolved all conflicted extension files (review.ts, hunchData.ts, extension.ts, package.json) to theirs and dropped the redundant webview. Both implementations honored the same invariant — the extension stays a pure reader and delegates every accept/reject/edit to the `hunch` CLI. Recorded to keep the graph true to the code that actually shipped.
decision: The review-queue draft-triage UI shipped, fulfilling dec_df253f50b4 — but via a PARALLEL implementation (remote commit 95dcc2b) that won a merge, not the standalone webview panel this session first built. The live design integrates drafts INTO the existing activity-bar tree (a 'Review queue' group with Ready-to-confirm / Needs-scrutiny sub-groups and per-draft leaves), with inline per-item actions Accept / Reject / Edit, plus batch Accept-all-verified / Reject-duplicates, an auto-review harness (hunch.autoReview), and a command hub (commands.ts / hunch.runCommand). review.ts is now a functional module (acceptDraft/rejectDraft/acceptVerified/rejectDuplicates/openDraftFile/draftBrief/autoReview) driving the tree, not a webview. Shipped as extension v0.4.0, installed locally.
consequences: The shipped review UI lives in the activity-bar tree, not a separate webview panel.
Inline draft EDITING now exists (hunch.editDraft) — the gap flagged in dec_92a6bacebf is closed by the merged build.
dec_92a6bacebf is superseded and no longer describes the live code.
The strategy veto (no standalone web app, dec_df253f50b4) remains live on its own topic.
alternatives_rejected: Keep BOTH the webview panel and the tree-integrated review — rejected: two review UIs is maintenance bloat with no added user value; the tree version is the more complete design.
Keep the standalone webview panel (this session's build) — rejected: the parallel tree-integrated build was a superset (inline Edit + auto-review harness), so the webview was dropped as redundant.
Let the extension write .hunch/ JSON directly — rejected: violates the Delegate-all-writes invariant; both implementations route writes through the CLI.
